title: Best-case comparison count of linear search
statement_informal: Linear search over a non-empty list performs at least one key comparison; this is the universal lower bound, achieved when the target is in the head position.

cost_model.notes: One x =? y test per element until a match; on a non-empty list at least the head is tested.

references: Classic; the best case of linear search is a constant 1 comparison.
